action.reasoning: Observation: No workspace files yet; need a fast baseline and immediate eval per instructions. Analysis: In AAV two_vs_many, mutations are concentrated in a 28-AA region, so a strong low-cost baseline is to infer the wild-type sequence from the most common residue at each position, featurize only mutation identities in the hotspot plus simple global counts, and train a small MLP regressor. This should train in seconds, validate the full pipeline, and produce an offline-safe checkpoint/predict script. Decision: Write a compact dataset/model/train/predict stack, train for a few epochs, test predict locally, and submit eval immediately.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 6 --batch_size 512 --hidden_dim 256 --dropout 0.1 --out checkpoints/baseline.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline.pt --output local_pred.json
